name: golang-any
title: golang-any
summary: Go programming language -- gccgo on "non-Go" platforms
description:
Go programming language -- gccgo on "non-Go" platforms
The Go programming language is an open source project to make programmers more
productive. Go is expressive, concise, clean, and efficient. Its concurrency
mechanisms make it easy to write programs that get the most out of multicore
and networked machines, while its novel type system enables flexible and
modular program construction. Go compiles quickly to machine code yet has the
convenience of garbage collection and the power of run-time reflection. It's a
fast, statically typed, compiled language that feels like a dynamically typed,
interpreted language.
.
This is a dependency package that depends on the default Go toolchain and
compiled standard library or gccgo on architectures that do not have a native
toolchain ("gc compiler") port. Because the gccgo and Go releases are not on
the same schedule, it is not guaranteed that the version of the Go language
provided by the version of gccgo that this package depends upon will match the
version of this package.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-any because Go programming language -- gccgo on "non-Go" platforms.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-any as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-any with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-any --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
